senses.gloss: to be in disorder, to be in great confusion, to be in great commotion
to be agitated, to be confused, to be excited
to handle or deal with clumsily
to make out with, to get busy with, to cohabit
to agitate, to disrupt, to put into (more) commotion, to stir up, to get (someone) excited
to jest, to play around (by being silly or the like), to clown around, to play the fool, to behave in a silly or foolish way
verbal noun of هَرَجَ (haraja) (form I), commotion, tumult

body.text: (TA:) in a trad. respecting the signs of the last day,
as explained by Mohammad himself: (S:) and so, accord to Aboo-Moosa. It signifies in the language of Abyssinia (TA.) Ibn-Keys Fr-Rukeiyát said in the days of the faction of Ibn-Ez-Zubayi.

layot $iEoriY A^aA^aw~alu Alharoji h`*aA
                      
                     
                        A^amN zamaAnN mino fitonapi gayori haroji
Would that I knew whether this be the first of the slaughter predicted as a sign of the last day
whether it be a time of trial,
other than the slaughter so predicted
